findings.problemShort: Banner must be a section element or have role="region"
findings.solutionShort: Use <section> element (implicit role) or add explicit role="region"
findings.solutionLong:
Primer accessibility requirement: The banner element <div> does not have the correct landmark role. Banner components must use a <section> element (which has an implicit role="region") or have an explicit role="region" attribute to be properly exposed as section landmarks for screen reader users.

TARGET_SELECTOR: `banner, [data-testid="banner"]`
TARGET_INDEX: 0

**Scanned page:** [https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html](https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html)

issue.title: Accessibility issue: Banner must be a section element or have role="region" on /a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html

pullRequest.title: [WIP] Fix accessibility issue with banner element

findings.problemShort: Banner section landmark missing accessible name
findings.solutionShort: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to provide context
findings.solutionLong:
Primer accessibility requirement: The banner element section[data-testid="banner"] is a section landmark, but does not have an accessible name. Section landmarks (<section> elements or role="region") require aria-label or aria-labelledby to provide context about the banner type (e.g., "Critical alert", "Info notification") for screen reader users.

TARGET_SELECTOR: `banner, [data-testid="banner"]`
TARGET_INDEX: 1

**Scanned page:** [https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html](https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html)

issue.title: Accessibility issue: Banner section landmark missing accessible name on /a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html

pullRequest.title: [WIP] Fix accessibility issue with banner section landmark

findings.problemShort: Banner icon must be marked as decorative with aria-hidden="true"
findings.solutionShort: Add aria-hidden="true" to the SVG icon element
findings.solutionLong:
Primer accessibility requirement: The banner section[data-testid="banner"] contains an SVG icon without aria-hidden="true". Banner type indicator icons are decorative because the banner type is conveyed through the accessible name and heading. Exposing the icon to screen readers creates redundant announcements. Add aria-hidden="true" to the SVG to hide it from assistive technologies. This relates to WCAG 2.1 Success Criteria 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

TARGET_SELECTOR: `banner, [data-testid="banner"]`
TARGET_INDEX: 2
TARGET_SELECTOR_MODE: svg

**Scanned page:** [https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html](https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html)

issue.title: Accessibility issue: Banner icon must be marked as decorative with aria-hidden="true" on /a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html

pullRequest.title: [WIP] Fix accessibility issue by marking banner icon as decorative

findings.problemShort: NavList must be a nav element or have role="navigation"
findings.solutionShort: Use <nav> element (implicit role) or add explicit role="navigation"
findings.solutionLong:
The navigation list element <div> does not have the correct landmark role. Navigation lists must use a <nav> element (which has an implicit role="navigation") or have an explicit role="navigation" attribute to be properly exposed as navigation landmarks for screen reader users. This allows users to quickly find and navigate to the navigation section. This violates WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).

TARGET_SELECTOR: `nav-list, [data-testid="nav-list"], [data-testid="repos-sidebar"]`
TARGET_INDEX: 0

issue.title: Accessibility issue: NavList must be a nav element or have role="navigation" on /a11y-scan-plugins/pages/aria-utils-test.html
